name: loky (Robust Process Pool Executor for Python)

performance.notes: Spawn-based multiprocessing; avoids fork() issues; worker startup overhead ~100ms

agent_readiness.notes: Robust drop-in replacement for concurrent.futures.ProcessPoolExecutor. from loky import get_reusable_executor. executor = get_reusable_executor(max_workers=4). future = executor.submit(my_func, arg). results = list(executor.map(my_func, args)). Reusable: worker processes persist between calls (no per-call startup). Crash recovery: workers respawn on crash. Cloudpickle serialization: supports lambda, closures, nested functions. Context: 'loky' (spawn), 'forkserver'. Used internally by joblib for scikit-learn parallel computation.

gotchas: Spawn-based (not fork) — slower startup but avoids fork() deadlocks
Workers are reused by default — state can leak between calls if not careful
Not async — use concurrent.futures or trio for async parallel execution
Worker restart on OOM or crash may hide underlying issues
best_when: CPU-bound parallelism where stdlib ProcessPoolExecutor has issues
Long-running worker pools where per-call startup cost is prohibitive
avoid_when: Simple one-shot parallelism without reusable workers
summary: loky provides a robust, reusable ProcessPoolExecutor with crash recovery and cloudpickle serialization. get_reusable_executor(max_workers=4).submit(func, arg). Workers persist between calls (no per-call startup). Supports lambda/closures via cloudpickle. Used internally by joblib/scikit-learn. BSD-3. Best when stdlib ProcessPoolExecutor has issues (fork deadlocks, no reuse, poor serialization). For most use cases, joblib wraps loky with better API.
